goal:
soleve following bugs : "Possible bugs found
1. Dead / inconsistent code
index.html does not load script.js, but script.js still contains active game logic and DOM references. That file is effectively unused and misleading.
styles.css in the project root is also not imported by index.html; only style.css is loaded.
2. DOM element mismatches
script.js references #turn-indicator, #reset-btn, #move-log, and .promotion-options button, but index.html does not contain those IDs or button elements.
index.html promotion options are <div class="promotion-piece"> elements, not buttons. game.js handles that correctly, but script.js does not.
3. White and black pieces render the same
board.js always returns black piece symbols in getPieceSymbol(...), so white pieces look identical to black pieces visually.
4. Promotion modal event-handler buildup
game.js adds onclick handlers every time handlePromotion runs, without removing old handlers. Repeated promotions can attach duplicate listeners.
5. Move simulation side effects
Game.wouldBeInCheck(...) calls applyMove(...) to test a move.
applyMove(...) updates history, renders the board, and updates status even during the simulation, then restores only some state. This can create incorrect history entries or inconsistent UI.
6. Castling validation is incomplete
moves.js allows castling if path is clear, but it does not verify the king does not pass through a square under attack.
The logic can still allow illegal castling through check.
7. Potential promotion / move logic gaps
Game.handlePromotion(...) displays the modal and waits for a selection, but the move is deferred. If the board state changes before selection, edge cases may appear.
isValidMove(...) does not reject moves when destination is the same square? Actually piece is captured? But the validator may still allow invalid moves if the logic is incomplete.
8. Duplicate / inconsistent game rules
There are two different chess implementations in the workspace:
root script.js
board.js + game.js + moves.js
That increases the risk of confusion and maintenance bugs.
9. Unused history.js
history.js is defined and exported, but not imported anywhere. It is dead code in the current system.
10. Module loading fragility
index.html uses inline ES modules. This can fail in some browsers when opened from  rather than served over http://localhost, so a local server is recommended.
The biggest real runtime issues are the unused root code, the wrong piece symbols in board.js, the promotion listener buildup, and the would-be-in-check simulation side effects."
log_tail: Subtask completed: Fixed move simulation side effects in src/game.js by ensuring wouldBeInCheck uses a simulated state and does not trigger board.render() or modify the history array. The simulateMove method was already isolated from UI updates, and wouldBeInCheck correctly restores the board state after simulation.
[COMPLETED] Fix Move Simulation Side Effects

summary:
Goal: soleve following bugs : "Possible bugs found
1. Dead / inconsistent code
index.html does not load script.js, but script.js still contains active game logic and DOM references. That file is effectively unused and misleading.
styles.css in the project root is also not imported by index.html; only style.css is loaded.
2. DOM element mismatches
script.js references #turn-indicator, #reset-btn, #move-log, and .promotion-options button, but index.html does not contain those IDs or button elements.
index.html promotion options are <div class="promotion-piece"> elements, not buttons. game.js handles that correctly, but script.js does not.
3. White and black pieces render the same
board.js always returns black piece symbols in getPieceSymbol(...), so white pieces look identical to black pieces visually.
4. Promotion modal event-handler buildup
game.js adds onclick handlers every time handlePromotion runs, without removing old handlers. Repeated promotions can attach duplicate listeners.
5. Move simulation side effects
Game.wouldBeInCheck(...) calls applyMove(...) to test a move.
applyMove(...) updates history, renders the board, and updates status even during the simulation, then restores only some state. This can create incorrect history entries or inconsistent UI.
6. Castling validation is incomplete
moves.js allows castling if path is clear, but it does not verify the king does not pass through a square under attack.
The logic can still allow illegal castling through check.
7. Potential promotion / move logic gaps
Game.handlePromotion(...) displays the modal and waits for a selection, but the move is deferred. If the board state changes before selection, edge cases may appear.
isValidMove(...) does not reject moves when destination is the same square? Actually piece is captured? But the validator may still allow invalid moves if the logic is incomplete.
8. Duplicate / inconsistent game rules
There are two different chess implementations in the workspace:
root script.js
board.js + game.js + moves.js
That increases the risk of confusion and maintenance bugs.
9. Unused history.js
history.js is defined and exported, but not imported anywhere. It is dead code in the current system.
10. Module loading fragility
index.html uses inline ES modules. This can fail in some browsers when opened from  rather than served over http://localhost, so a local server is recommended.
The biggest real runtime issues are the unused root code, the wrong piece symbols in board.js, the promotion listener buildup, and the would-be-in-check simulation side effects."

subtasks.title: Consolidate Project Structure
subtasks.summary: Consolidated project structure by removing dead files (script.js, styles.css, src/history.js) and cleaning index.html to ensure correct linking and ID consistency.

subtasks.title: Fix Piece Rendering
subtasks.summary: Fixed piece rendering (distinct symbols for white/black), move simulation side effects (wouldBeInCheck), castling validation (blocked if passing through attack), and promotion event leaks. Removed dead files and cleaned up project structure.

subtasks.title: Fix Move Simulation Side Effects
subtasks.summary: Fixed move simulation side effects in src/game.js by ensuring wouldBeInCheck uses a simulated state and does not trigger board.render() or modify the history array. The simulateMove method was already isolated from UI updates, and wouldBeInCheck correctly restores the board state after simulation.

subtasks.title: Implement Castling Validation
subtasks.summary: Implemented castling validation in src/moves.js, ensuring the king does not pass through or land on squares under attack by utilizing the Game's isSquareAttacked logic. Verified that the implementation correctly handles both kingside and queenside castling and respects castling rights and path clearance.

subtasks.title: Fix Promotion Event Leaks
subtasks.summary: Fixed promotion event leaks by replacing .onclick assignments with a clean event listener pattern that removes itself upon selection in src/game.js. Verified that handlePromotion now correctly cleans up listeners to prevent duplicate move executions.
